title: Question about Platinum Collection
wikitext:
[]: Question about Platinum Collection             Quote Post by Kurairush » Tue Jan 22, 2013 10:27 pm I was considering adding Evangelion to my slow growing collection of anime DVDs and I have a question. Does the English dub on this have subtitles for the Japanese text that shows up on screen? I know Episodes 25 and 26 have a few things related to character development that are in Japanese text. I'm just asking, since I might show this anime to my dad, but I would rather watch the dub with him, than to watch it subbed.(I can watch subtitled anime just fine, but I'm not sure about his case, considering that any anime I've watched with him were dubbed.) Also, should I skip ahead to End of Evangelion after episode 24 when I watch it with my dad or should we just watch all 26 episodes, with End of Evangelion treated as a view of what's happening during or after episodes 25 & 26?

[]:             Quote Post by Maya Ibuki » Tue Jan 22, 2013 11:35 pm Yes, you can view it in English with subtitles for the Japanese text (Softsubs will appear beside any Japanese text) Preferably you should watch the entire series - including 25 and 26, then go onto watching The End Of Evangelion. There is much debate between the two endings, however many see EoE as a more literal ending (Some even go as far to say that 25 and 26 is merely Shinji's subconciousness, and that EoE is actually the situation outside of his mind - a las in real life.)

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Tue Jan 22, 2013 11:36 pm Yes to the subtitles for Japanese text, and for the second bit, watch 21-24 director's cut, then skip to EoE. At least, that's what I do.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jan 23, 2013 9:35 am Watch 21-26, then treat 21'-26' as an alternate ending.

Does the English dub on this have subtitles for the Japanese text that shows up on screen? I know Episodes 25 and 26 have a few things related to character development that are in Japanese text. I'm just asking, since I might show this anime to my dad, but I would rather watch the dub with him, than to watch it subbed.(I can watch subtitled anime just fine, but I'm not sure about his case, considering that any anime I've watched with him were dubbed.) Also, should I skip ahead to End of Evangelion after episode 24 when I watch it with my dad or should we just watch all 26 episodes, with End of Evangelion treated as a view of what's happening during or after episodes 25 & 26?
